rulings.Case Title: RODNEY JACKSON II VS. BAUER'S INTELLIGENT TRANSPORTATION, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Defendant/Appellants Appeal Of The Order, Decision Or Award And To Enter Judgment Against Defendant/Appellant
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 8, 2024, Line 15, PLAINTIFF RODNEY JACKSON's Motion To Dismiss Defendant/Appellants Appeal Of The Order, Decision Or Award And To Enter Judgment Against Defendant/Appellant. (The full tentative ruling is emailed to the parties). Plaintiff's motion to dismiss, enter judgment, and obtain fees is granted. Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RCE).

rulings.Case Title: ED VAN RODEN VS. MARGIE ROGERSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Mediation, Request For Stay Alternatively A Dismissal Of Proceedings; **First Appearance Fees Paid**
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 8, 2024, Line 13, DEFENDANT MARGIE ROGERSON's Motion To Compel Mediation, Request For Stay Alternatively A Dismissal Of Proceedings. Defendant's motion to compel mediation is denied. In Jeld-Wen, Inc. v. Superior Court (2007) 146 Cal.App.4th 536, the court held that courts lack the power to order parties to mediation given the completely voluntary nature of the process. Under CRC 3.853, a "mediator must(2) [r]espect the right of each participant to decide the extent of his or her participation in the mediation, including the right to withdraw from the mediation at any time; and (3) [r]efrain from coercing any party to make a decision or to continue to participate in the mediation." The record is clear that plaintiff no longer wants to mediate. It would be a feckless order to compel plaintiff to mediate since he is free to withdraw at anytime. Given this backdrop, the motion is denied. (But see Breslin v. Breslin (2021) 62 Cal.App.5th 801, 806 [referring to a motion to compel mediation in the context of a probate action with little analysis].) Lange v. Schilling (2008) 163 Cal.App.4th 1412, 1416-1417 is distinguishable. There, the court held that if the contract bars recovery of attorney fees by a prevailing party who commences an action without first attempting to resolve the matter through mediation or who refuses to mediate after a request has been made, that provision will be enforced and attorney fees that would otherwise have been available will not be awarded. Lange does not stand for the proposition that the court can compel mediation. An order compelling mediation is antithetical to the entire concept of voluntary mediation. Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RCE).

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND CHENG ET AL VS. BOSA DEVELOPMENT CALIFORNIA II, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 8, 2024, Line 11, DEFENDANT ARDEN OWNERS ASSOCIATION's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Continued to November 22, 2024, to allow opposing party the opportunity to provide courtesy copies, per SF Local Rule 2.7B, Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work. =(302/RCE).

rulings.Case Title: ZAIRE HILL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 8, 2024, Line 12, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. Transferred to be heard in department 501 on November 18, 2024. That department handles all cases alleging a breach of lease per SF Local Rule 8.10A1. Note, the court lacks courtesy copies of the City's moving papers. The City shall submit those courtesy copies to department 501 no later than November 12, 2024. Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work. =(302/RCE).
